name: Shortcut Creator
description: A utility for creating and deploying desktop shortcuts through Microsoft Intune as Win32 applications. Generates .intunewin files, detection scripts, and deployment instructions automatically. Supports URL, file path, and Microsoft Store app shortcuts with custom icons.
keywords: how to deploy desktop shortcut with intune
troubleshoot shortcut not appearing on desktop
install desktop link via win32 app
deploy url link to all users
author: Shehab Noaman

securityCheck.aiSummary: The Sync-IntuneDeviceCategories.ps1 script appears to be a standard Intune Graph API integration for device categorization with no obfuscated code, credential harvesting, or data exfiltration evident. However, the installation flow (Install.ps1) and the shutdown-based rename script (Rename-Device-on-Shutdown.ps1) introduce persistence and potentially disruptive behavior (automatic renaming) that could be misused for unauthorized control. If these are legitimate admin tools, ensure governance: document purpose, restrict scope, obtain approvals, add auditing, and provide safe-guards and cleanup steps. No hardcoded secrets detected; Graph API usage aligns with expected flows for Intune management.
